title: vouacapoua retusa
intro: Vouacapoua retusa, also known as the Vouacapoua tree, is a plant species native to tropical Africa, particularly in the countries of the Congo Basin. The plant's bark, roots, and leaves are rich in medicinal properties, containing compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, which exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Vouacapoua retusa include its potential to lower blood pressure, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of malaria and other infections, as well as its use in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions. In herbal preparations, the bark and roots of the Vouacapoua tree are typically dried, ground into a powder, and used as a medicinal tea or infusion.
